nodes.label: UniMelb Current Students IA Project
nodes.description: A service design / UX research project analyzing the current student digital experience across the University of Melbourne's 10+ topic areas, using cross-faculty web crawl data to inform information architecture improvements.

nodes.label: Hub-and-Spoke IA Architecture
nodes.description: The proposed information architecture model where the central hub (students.unimelb.edu.au) owns cross-cutting transactional services while faculties maintain discipline-specific content, connected via hub gateway pages.

nodes.label: Cross-Faculty Content Analysis
nodes.description: The primary research methodology: a full web crawl of all faculty current-student websites, tagged by topic area, with per-topic deep-dive analysis identifying distribution, content types, legitimate vs hub-overlapping content, and recommendations.

nodes.label: Central Student Hub (students.unimelb.edu.au)
nodes.description: The University's central current-student portal providing transactional services including My Course Planner, the Handbook, enrolment, timetabling, LMS/Canvas, wellbeing, careers, and academic skills support.

nodes.label: My Course Planner
nodes.description: Central University course planning tool owned by the hub. Currently re-hosted by FEIT as a faculty-specific page rather than linked to, representing a hub overlap pattern.

nodes.label: University Handbook
nodes.description: The canonical University Handbook providing subject and course information. Faculty content often duplicates Handbook content rather than linking to it.

nodes.label: Candidature Management System
nodes.description: The central system driving the graduate research candidature lifecycle — commencement, progress reviews, milestones, variations, conversion, and thesis submission. Faculties re-narrate this system's processes rather than linking to the hub spine.

nodes.label: Sonia Placement Tracking System
nodes.description: The placement-tracking system used by MDHS for clinical placement compliance and management. Faculty-owned but centrally significant.

nodes.label: scholarships.unimelb.edu.au
nodes.description: The central University scholarships catalogue and application system. Arts already links directly to it, but the hub currently holds zero scholarship pages in the crawl.

nodes.label: LMS / Canvas
nodes.description: The central learning management system. Faculty content about LMS communities (e.g. Law's Legal Academic Skills LMS Community) is legitimate; generic LMS instructions should be hub-owned.

nodes.label: Student IT Helpdesk
nodes.description: Central IT support service. Multiple faculties re-front this service (FEIT 'Student IT', 'Student IT Contact', 'Chat with Student IT'; Education 'Student Services and IT') rather than linking to the hub.

nodes.label: Academic Skills Unit (Central)
nodes.description: The University's central Academic Skills service providing workshops, drop-ins, and study-skills support. Multiple faculties maintain near-identical 'Academic Skills' landing pages duplicating this service.

nodes.label: Careers & Employability Service (Central)
nodes.description: The University's central careers service including Careers Online, appointment booking, resume/cover-letter advice. Seven faculties each maintain an 'Employability in X' landing page duplicating this service.

nodes.label: Student Equity & Disability Support (SEDS)
nodes.description: The central University equity and disability support function. Multiple faculties restate this service locally; only building accessibility and equity-themed clinics/subjects are genuinely faculty-owned.

nodes.label: Murrup Barak (Indigenous Student Support)
nodes.description: The University's central Indigenous student support hub. Four faculties maintain separate 'Indigenous student' landing pages that should consolidate behind this hub anchor.

nodes.label: Content Duplication Pattern
nodes.description: A pervasive finding across all 14 topics: faculties independently maintain near-identical pages for cross-cutting services (academic skills, careers, wellbeing, equity, IT support, club administration) that the hub already owns, resulting in duplicated maintenance burden and fragmented student experience.

nodes.label: URL Churn & Migration Artefacts
nodes.description: Many topic page counts are inflated by URL migration duplicates (e.g., FEIT's progress-your-career to student-experience redirects), _recache URL twins (Law JD/MLM subjects), _nocache mirrors (Arts), and per-year page accretion (Dean's Honours Lists, scholarship rounds).

nodes.label: Link-Farm & Stub Page Pattern
nodes.description: A recurring low-value content pattern: sub-100-word pages that exist only as navigational scaffolding pointing to other pages, inflating topic counts without providing substantive content. Especially prevalent in Law and Arts.

nodes.label: Topic Tagging Noise
nodes.description: Several topics are heavily inflated by false positives: FEIT's entire estate tagged as IT-systems due to domain name; orientation topic absorbing 160+ non-orientation pages under FEIT's /orientation/ URL tree; inclusion-equity inflated by GRD bios and studio archives; graduation conflating coursework completion with HDR candidature.

nodes.label: Legitimate vs Hub-Overlapping Content Classification
nodes.description: The core analytical framework: each topic is split into (a) genuinely faculty-owned content that cannot be centralised (discipline-specific sample plans, regulated career pathways, named clubs, clinical placements) and (b) hub-overlapping content that duplicates central services (generic academic skills, careers, wellbeing, IT support, enrolment mechanics).

nodes.label: Hub Gateway Page Pattern
nodes.description: The primary IA recommendation: for each topic, add a thin hub gateway page (e.g., students.unimelb.edu.au/your-course/faculty-course-planning) that links to every faculty's discipline-specific content, making it discoverable from the centre. Currently faculty content is invisible from the hub.

nodes.label: Standardise Faculty Entry Pages
nodes.description: Recommendation that each faculty exposes a consistently-labelled page for each topic area (e.g., 'Plan your course', 'Clubs & student life', 'Forms & admin'), following a standard shape with links to both faculty-specific content and hub services.

nodes.label: Course Planning Topic
nodes.description: 207 pages — third-largest topic. Dominated by degree-specific sample course plans (MBS holds 58 pages). Mostly legitimate faculty-owned content; the duplication is the thinner top layer of generic planning advice and re-hosted tools.

nodes.label: Academic Skills Topic
nodes.description: 37 pages — smallest topic. Pattern is split and thin: Law's Ian Malkin Centre is legitimately discipline-specific; the rest is near-identical 'Academic Skills' landing pages and peer-mentoring re-runs that overlap the central Academic Skills unit.

nodes.label: Careers & Employability Topic
nodes.description: 141 pages — most evenly distributed topic. Every faculty independently maintains an 'Employability in X' landing page and its own career-mentoring program. Genuine faculty content is regulated career pathways and credit-bearing internship subjects.

nodes.label: Clubs & Events Topic
nodes.description: 104 pages — most clearly faculty-owned topic. Named discipline clubs, conservatorium ensembles, flagship competitions, and HDR community are irreducibly local. Duplication is thin and concentrated in club-administration toolkits.

nodes.label: Contacts & Support Topic
nodes.description: 177 pages — most diffuse topic. Almost none genuinely discipline-specific; it is the long tail of faculty restatements of cross-cutting services (wellbeing, equity, IT, careers, mentoring). Arts and Law hold 49% of pages, much of it link-farms and duplicates.

nodes.label: Forms & Admin Topic
nodes.description: 212 pages — most heterogeneous topic. The catch-all administrative spine: offer acceptance, enrolment, extensions, compliance checks, forms hubs. MDHS's 36 near-identical pre-placement compliance pages and Law's 66 transactional pages are half the topic.

nodes.label: Graduation Topic
nodes.description: 125 pages — the tag is a near-total misnomer. Almost none is about the ceremony; it captures the end-of-degree lifecycle: research-candidature milestones, honours, prizes. Arts holds 49%. Heavy with link-farms and path-variant duplicates.

nodes.label: Inclusion & Equity Topic
nodes.description: 49 pages — one of the smallest topics, heavily inflated by false positives. Only ~13 pages are genuinely about inclusion/equity (Indigenous student support, disability access, equity clinics). ~22 pages are keyword-match noise.

nodes.label: International Student Support Topic
nodes.description: 135 pages — label mismatch: content is overwhelmingly outbound global mobility (study abroad, degree partnerships, travelling studios), not inbound international student support. Three units hold 80%. Genuine welfare/compliance content is hub-owned and largely absent from faculties.

nodes.label: IT & Systems Topic
nodes.description: 235 pages — but 78% are FEIT, and most are not about IT at all (internships, scholarships, clubs swept in by the eng.unimelb.edu.au domain). The genuine IT signal is ~30-40 genuinely IT-flavoured pages spread thinly across other units.

nodes.label: Orientation Topic
nodes.description: 289 pages — deceptively large count, almost entirely a FEIT tagging artifact (204 of 289 pages because its whole estate nests under /orientation/ paths). Genuine orientation footprint is small and legitimately faculty-owned: per-degree welcome landings.

nodes.label: Placements & Work-Integrated Learning Topic
nodes.description: 206 pages — most lopsided topic: MDHS holds 47% (clinical compliance spine). Overwhelmingly faculty-owned and legitimately so — placements are bound to professional accreditation, host relationships, and credit-bearing subjects.

nodes.label: Research Candidature Topic
nodes.description: 241 pages — third-largest topic. Almost entirely faculty-owned. Law holds 36% but mostly ~70 individual graduate-researcher profile pages. The candidature lifecycle spine is the same journey everywhere, driven by one central system.

nodes.label: Scholarships Topic
nodes.description: 141 pages — mid-sized. FEIT alone holds 50%. Heavily faculty-owned by default rather than design: named awards are legitimate; rounds, T&Cs, FAQs, Honours Lists, and recipient galleries are generic process scaffolding suited for centralisation.

nodes.label: Faculty of Engineering & IT (FEIT)
nodes.description: The faculty with the most lopsided content distribution in the audit. Holds 78% of IT-systems tag (domain artefact), 71% of orientation (URL path artefact), 50% of scholarships. Major source of URL churn from progress-your-career to student-experience migration.

nodes.label: Melbourne Law School
nodes.description: Second-largest page holder across many topics. Inflated by ~70 individual graduate-researcher profile pages, extensive degree partnership pages, and many link-farm stubs. Holds the genuinely discipline-specific Ian Malkin Centre for Legal Academic Skills.

nodes.label: Faculty of Arts
nodes.description: Major content holder with heavy duplication: multiple _nocache and path-variant URLs for the same pages (Graduate Research Orientation ×3, Overview of course structure ×3, etc.). Holds 49% of graduation topic and 28% of contacts-support.

nodes.label: Faculty of Medicine, Dentistry & Health Sciences (MDHS)
nodes.description: Holds 47% of placements/WIL topic with its clinical compliance spine (36 near-identical pre-placement check pages). The most genuinely faculty-owned content in the audit due to professional accreditation requirements.

nodes.label: Melbourne Business School (MBS)
nodes.description: A school within FBE that holds 58 course-planning pages — 28% of the topic — almost entirely one sample course plan per master's program. Represents the school-level fragmentation pattern seen elsewhere.

nodes.label: Architecture, Building & Planning / Melbourne School of Design (MSD)
nodes.description: Major holder of course-planning (29 pages of double-masters plans), research-candidature (43 pages of thesis/research studios + archives), and clubs-events (MSDx exhibition apparatus). Maintains deep studio archives dating back to 2014.

nodes.label: Faculty of Fine Arts & Music (FFAM)
nodes.description: Holds the largest single IT-tagged page (Tech Support and Facilities, 3,501w) and unique conservatorium content (ensembles, audition/registration, instrument loans). Content is strongly faculty-owned due to physical discipline kit.

nodes.label: Faculty of Education
nodes.description: Major holder of placements/WIL content (24 pages of ITE professional experience machinery) and careers-employability (23 pages of teaching placement/registration content). Genuinely faculty-owned due to teacher accreditation requirements.

nodes.label: Faculty of Science
nodes.description: Holds 26 course-planning pages (per-major 'getting started' guides) and 13 scholarship pages. Maintains a well-structured scholarship catalogue but duplicates generic FAQ and process content.

nodes.label: School of Biomedical Sciences (MDHS)
nodes.description: Holds 37 course-planning pages and 34 forms-admin pages. Major source of redirect noise (16 redirect stubs to study.unimelb major pages). Maintains two parallel 'Academic Skills' pages for UG and master's.

nodes.label: Faculty of Business & Economics (FBE)
nodes.description: Smaller holder across topics but notable for duplicated IT content (computing spaces/rules/virtual lab each exist twice under /bcom/ and /services/), and for having only 2 SSO-redirect stubs in careers.

nodes.label: Ian Malkin Centre for Legal Academic Skills
nodes.description: A named, discipline-specific academic skills teaching service at Melbourne Law School with its own staff and LMS community. The clearest example of genuinely irreducible faculty-owned academic skills content.

nodes.label: 'Employability in X' Landing Pages
nodes.description: Seven near-identical faculty landing pages (Law, Arts, Science, FEIT, MDHS, FFAM, Education) restating the central careers service. The textbook duplication pattern identified in the careers-employability topic.

nodes.label: Generic 'Academic Skills' Landing Pages
nodes.description: Five-plus near-identical landing pages (FEIT ×2, Biomedical ×2, Education, FBE 'Academic Success') restating the central Academic Skills unit service. A clearest consolidation target.

nodes.label: MDHS Pre-Placement Compliance Sprawl
nodes.description: 36 near-identical per-check definition pages (WWCC, police check, immunisations, NDIS, etc.) in MDHS's forms-admin topic. Each uses the same 'What is it? / What do I need? / How long?' template. The generic check definitions are hub-overlapping; only the course-specific 'which checks does my degree need' layer is faculty-owned.

nodes.label: Graduate Researcher Profile Pages
nodes.description: ~70 individual Law PhD candidate bio pages and FEIT's single 'Meet our graduate researchers' roster page. The single biggest driver of research-candidature topic page count, recommended for conversion to a filterable directory rather than standalone pages.

nodes.label: Dean's Honours List Yearly Accretion
nodes.description: A recurring pattern where one new page is created per year for Dean's Honours Lists (FEIT: 2022/2023/2024; Science: 2023/2024; MSD: 2023/2024) and scholarship winner galleries. Marketing/recognition content, not current-student services.

nodes.label: Placement Lifecycle Template Pattern
nodes.description: MDHS repeats a 'Before placement / During placement / Conclusion of placement / Day one' structure across every clinical department. The repetition is structural (each department's content differs), not redundant duplication — but it drives high page counts.

nodes.label: Peer Mentoring / PASS Duplication
nodes.description: Peer-assisted study (PASS) and peer mentoring programs are described near-identically across multiple faculties (Arts, Education, Law, MDHS, MSD). The same model, separately documented per faculty. Standardisation as one university program with a hub page is recommended.

nodes.label: Indigenous Student Support Duplication
nodes.description: Four separate faculty 'Indigenous student' landing pages (Science, MDHS, FEIT, MBS) serving as parallel reinventions of the central Murrup Barak hub. Each is bespoke to its discipline but none links to a common hub anchor.

nodes.label: Outbound Mobility vs Inbound Support Mismatch
nodes.description: The international topic label implies inbound support for international students, but the content is overwhelmingly outbound global mobility (exchange, degree partnerships, travelling studios). Genuine inbound welfare/compliance (visas, OSHC, ESOS) is hub-owned and absent from faculties.

nodes.label: Graduation Tag Conflating Two Journeys
nodes.description: The graduation tag conflates coursework degree completion (ceremony, parchment) with HDR candidature lifecycle (milestones, thesis, examination) — two different journeys with different owners. Recommendation: split into separate IA topics.

nodes.label: FEIT Dual Event Calendar Paths
nodes.description: FEIT's event calendar exists at two parallel URL paths (/students/eng-and-it-calendar/... and /students/coursework/student-experience/orientation/.../eng-and-it-calendar/...), causing most calendar entries to appear twice.

nodes.label: Arts Path-Variant & _nocache Duplication
nodes.description: Arts carries multiple identical pages under both /commencement/ and /plan-your-program2/ paths plus _nocache twins. Examples: Graduate Research Orientation ×3, Overview of course structure ×3, Commencement form checklist ×2.

nodes.label: FBE IT Content Duplication
nodes.description: FBE's computing-spaces, facilities rules, and virtual lab pages each exist twice — once under /bcom/ and once under /services/ — representing unnecessary within-faculty duplication.

nodes.label: Content Type Classification Framework
nodes.description: Each deep-dive classifies pages into 7-9 content types (e.g., degree-specific sample plans, discipline-specific centres, generic landing pages, link-farm gateways, mis-tagged pages) enabling systematic comparison across faculties and topics.

nodes.label: Per-Faculty Page Distribution Analysis
nodes.description: Each deep-dive includes a distribution table showing which faculty holds how many pages in the topic and what content type dominates their share, enabling identification of concentration and fragmentation patterns.

nodes.label: Full Faculty Web Crawl Dataset
nodes.description: The primary data source: a complete crawl of all faculty current-student websites conducted June 2026, with pages tagged by topic area, word counts, URL classification (redirect, link-farm, mixed), and deduplication.

hyperedges.description: The academic skills topic requires: (1) hub to own the canonical Academic Skills service as single source of truth for generic study-skills content; (2) faculties to keep only discipline-bound content (Law's Ian Malkin Centre, Science academic English, thesis-writing); (3) collapse five-plus near-identical 'Academic Skills' landing pages; (4) standardise peer mentoring/PASS as one university program with a hub page; (5) publish hub gateway at students.unimelb.edu.au/academic-skills/by-faculty.

The careers topic requires: (1) hub to own canonical Careers & Employability service (appointment booking, resume advice, job-search strategy); (2) faculties to keep regulated career pathways (Law admission, Education registration) and credit-bearing internship subjects; (3) collapse seven 'Employability in X' pages into one hub page plus thin per-faculty stubs; (4) kill URL churn (FEIT's 5× ICMP, 4× Professional Skills Series); (5) publish hub gateway at students.unimelb.edu.au/careers/faculty-employability.

The clubs/events topic requires: (1) hub to own generic club-administration spine (room booking, event funding, promotion); (2) faculties to keep named clubs, ensembles, flagship competitions, mentoring/ambassador programs, and HDR community; (3) collapse FEIT's dual calendar paths; (4) add hub gateway at students.unimelb.edu.au/your-community/faculty-clubs-and-events; (5) standardise single 'Clubs & student life' entry page per faculty.

The contacts/support topic requires: (1) hub to own cross-cutting safety net as single source of truth (wellbeing, equity/disability, emergency contacts, academic skills, careers); (2) faculties to keep IT/tech support for discipline kit, studio facilities, discipline mentoring, placement-specific help; (3) add standard 'Get help / key contacts' page per faculty; (4) stop tagging individual people and duplicate URLs; (5) retire faculty restatements of wellbeing/equity/careers in favour of hub links.

The forms/admin topic requires: (1) hub to own transactional spine (Get Started, enrolment, extensions, special consideration) as single source of truth; (2) hub to own generic check definitions (WWCC, police check, immunisation); MDHS to keep only course-specific 'which checks' layer; (3) faculties to keep forms hubs, discipline milestones, conduct rules, and bespoke programmes with standard 'Forms & admin' page; (4) add hub gateway at students.unimelb.edu.au/admin/faculty-forms; (5) clean _recache twins and 0w form-target redirects.

The graduation topic requires: (1) hub to own graduation spine outright (eligibility, apply-to-graduate, ceremony logistics, parchments); (2) faculties to keep discipline-bound completion content (milestones, thesis submission, honours/awards, placement conclusion); (3) split the tag: separate 'coursework completion' from 'HDR candidature' as different journeys; (4) clean Arts/FEIT noise (link-farms, redirects, path-variant duplicates); (5) add hub gateway linking to central apply-to-graduate and each faculty's honours/awards pages.

The inclusion/equity topic requires: (1) hub to own inclusion/equity spine (SEDS page, Murrup Barak) as single source of truth; (2) faculties to keep only irreducibly local content (building accessibility, equity-themed clinics/subjects, D&I events, discipline-specific Indigenous programs); (3) fix the tag: ~22 of 49 pages are false positives (GRD bios, MSD studios, music timetable); (4) consolidate four faculty 'Indigenous student' pages behind one hub anchor; (5) add hub gateway listing each faculty's Indigenous-support contact and equity liaison.

The international topic requires: (1) hub to own inbound international-student support outright (visas, OSHC, ESOS/CoE, work rights, arrival/orientation); (2) faculties to keep global mobility content (degree partnerships, exchange partner lists, international research placements, travelling studios, dual degrees); (3) re-label and split: separate 'outbound mobility' (faculty) from 'inbound support' (hub) as different IA topics; (4) clean Law's tag (~25 GRD profiles and link-farm stubs); (5) add hub gateway at students.unimelb.edu.au/study-and-exchange/global-opportunities.

The IT/systems topic requires: (1) hub to own IT spine outright (Student IT helpdesk, software/device entitlements, password/account/email, LMS, MyTimetable, SSO, building access); (2) faculties to keep only physical IT (computing labs, studio/AV/instrument loans, local IT-support notes); (3) stop re-fronting Student IT (retire FEIT's Student IT/Contact/Chat trio and Education's helpdesk restatement); (4) fix the tag: re-classify FEIT's non-IT pages (internships, scholarships, clubs) to their true topics; (5) de-duplicate FBE's computing-spaces/rules/virtual-lab set.

The orientation topic requires: (1) hub to own welcome spine (University-wide Orientation week dates, pre-arrival checklist, IT/software setup, international onboarding); (2) faculties to keep dated per-degree orientation event pages with cohort dates/venues and peer-mentoring links; (3) fix the tagging: FEIT's /orientation/ URL tree should not parent scholarships, clubs, and internships; (4) add hub gateway at students.unimelb.edu.au/new-students/orientation; (5) retire faculty 'accept your offer' checklist duplicates in favour of linking the hub checklist.

The placements/WIL topic requires: (1) hub to own only cross-cutting wrappers (canonical insurance, travel/visas, IP/rights, 'What is WIL?' explainer); (2) faculties to keep everything discipline-bound (compliance gates, credit-bearing internship subjects, clinics, ITE professional experience, host-organisation content); (3) add hub gateway at students.unimelb.edu.au/your-course/work-integrated-learning; (4) set placement-page standard so every faculty's WIL hub exposes same shape (credit-bearing, eligibility, how to apply, compliance, host info); (5) clean FEIT's internship URL migration and MDHS's 404/stubs.

The research candidature topic requires: (1) hub to own candidature spine (commencement, milestone/progress reviews, variations, conversion, thesis submission) sourced from the Candidature Management System; (2) faculties to keep discipline layer (milestone specifics, research studios, faculty-specific funding, research-project subjects); (3) convert ~70 individual Law graduate-researcher bios to a filterable directory; (4) add hub gateway at students.unimelb.edu.au/.../graduate-research/faculty-requirements; (5) consolidate duplicated candidature lifecycle prose into one hub-owned spine.

The scholarships topic requires: (1) hub to own the spine: promote scholarships.unimelb.edu.au as canonical catalogue + application engine with parameterised eligibility, rounds, T&Cs, and FAQs; (2) faculties to keep named/donor awards and discipline-specific HDR funding, deep-linking to central system; (3) de-duplicate FEIT (collapse two parallel URL trees and year-by-year Honours/Spotlight churn); (4) set recognition standard: move Dean's Honours Lists and winners' galleries from services IA to news/alumni; (5) add hub gateway at students.unimelb.edu.au/your-finances/scholarships.

The course planning topic requires: (1) hub to own My Course Planner, the Handbook, and general planning advice as single source of truth; (2) faculties to own degree-specific sample plans, major subject-selection guides, honours, research-project and registration-bound planning; (3) add hub gateway at students.unimelb.edu.au/your-course/faculty-course-planning; (4) stop re-hosting the tool (FEIT's My Course Planner page should link, not reproduce); (5) standardise entry pattern: each faculty exposes a 'Plan your course' page with consistent label and location.

Across multiple topics, tagging quality is the highest-value, lowest-cost fix: (1) IT-systems: re-classify FEIT's 183 non-IT pages; (2) orientation: re-home FEIT's 160+ non-orientation pages; (3) inclusion-equity: remove ~22 false positives; (4) graduation: split coursework completion from HDR candidature; (5) international: split outbound mobility from inbound support; (6) remove _recache, _nocache, and redirect stubs across all topics.
